strengths: Hands-on AI-agent infrastructure builder: at re:cinq delivered MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — exactly the 'AI-powered product development' and 'builder, code, and design fluency' the role calls for.

~17 years of continuous professional engineering across web, mobile, embedded, distributed high-throughput systems (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP req/s), and cloud-native stacks — meets and slightly exceeds the 15-year minimum with unusually broad platform coverage.

Demonstrated entrepreneurial track record: founded GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform, Kubernetes-deployable, live users including Green Map), spoke at FOSS4G, built and maintained open-source OSS libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial, Vibe.dav) — matching the 'startup experience' and 'inspire others externally' dimensions.
concerns: No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field — the role's preferred academic credential is absent, which may matter when advising strategic customers who expect credentialed domain authority.
Active career gap since October 2025 (~9 months at time of application) with no stated project or research activity listed, leaving recent trajectory ambiguous for a director-level IC role.

Current and prior titles (Staff / Senior Software Engineer) reflect strong individual-contributor work but not yet the principal/director-level scope — cross-org influence and strategic customer advising at that seniority is not evidenced in the resume.
response.justification: Bogdan brings rare practical depth in the AI-agent tooling layer — MCP frameworks, agentic workflow infrastructure — at a moment when most candidates can only theorize about it; this is precisely the 'recognized expertise with demonstrated products built with AI' the role prizes. Layered on top is 17 years of polyglot, cross-domain engineering (high-throughput data pipelines, cloud-native infra, embedded systems, open-source platforms) that validates the breadth a principal-level AI strategy advisor must draw on when working with diverse engineering and product teams. The founding of GISCollective and history of public teaching (FOSS4G talk, multi-module Selenium course, Web Internals workshop) show the 'inspire others internally and externally' mandate is already lived practice, not an aspiration — making him a compelling candidate to advance despite the gap in formal AI credentials.
